observations.recommendation:
1.17. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the:

a. Inventory Committee to conduct complete annual physical count of
all Property, Plant and Equipment as required in Volume 1,
Chapter 7 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs; and promptly
prepare the Report thereon and furnish the Office of the Auditor not
later than January 31st of each year;

b. Municipal Accountant and the designated property custodian to
maintain separate PPE records and reconcile those records with the
RPCPPE in order to establish the accurate PPE account balances in
the financial statements; and

c. Municipal Accountant to prepare the necessary adjusting entries to
reclassify the misclassified PPE amounting to ₱3.327 million to its
appropriate account.

2.18. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the
Municipal Accountant to:

a. Implement a computerized depreciation schedule for all depreciable
assets and ensure proper allocation of depreciation in line with
IPSAS 17;

b. Update the depreciation schedule with detailed information on PPE
to facilitate the verification and analysis of relevant accounts, and
accurate tracking of depreciation expenses for each Property, Plant,
and Equipment (PPE) account class; and

c. Reconcile accumulated depreciation account balances with the
depreciation schedules and make the necessary adjustments to
correct the accumulated depreciation and other affected accounts in
the financial statements, ensuring compliance with IPSAS 17.

3.19. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to undertake the
following actions:

a. Request copy of bank statements or snapshots from the servicing
banks to facilitate the preparation and submission of the monthly
BRS within the prescribed period;

b. Verify the existence of the account with a balance of ₱1,300.00 with
the servicing bank to ensure that there will be a basis in closing the
account in the books;

c. Revisit and review transactions of the previous years, exhaust all
available means to obtain supporting documents, and promptly
record long-standing reconciling items;

d. Prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) for the necessary
corrections and adjustments for the duly verified reconciling items;
and

e. Strengthen coordination among their departments to ensure the
timely submission of documents and recording of transactions for
the Cash in Bank-Local Currency Cash Account (LCCA), thereby
improving the reliability and accuracy of financial reporting.

4.14. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the
Municipal Accountant to:

a. Exert best effort to gather documents and identify the source
agencies relative to the dormant residual fund transfers;

b. Prepare the corresponding aging of Due to NGAs and LGUs
accounts and present the same in the Notes to Financial Statements;
and

c. Prepare appropriate adjusting entries to correct the Due to LGUs
account, amounting to ₱185,442.41.

4.15. We further recommended and the Municipal Treasurer agreed to
refund the unused/residual balances of fund transfers or seek written
approval from the source agencies to utilize the balances for other
specific purposes.

5.27. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the:

a. Municipal Accountant to record regular purchases of supplies and
materials in the appropriate inventory accounts, and issuances
thereof shall be recorded as they occur, utilizing the perpetual
inventory method;

b. Custodians of supplies to prepare the RIS for the issuances of
supplies and materials and accordingly consolidate the same using
the SSMI/RSMI and submit them to the Accounting Office as basis
to record corresponding expenses in the books of accounts;

c. Municipal Accountant maintain General Ledger per fund, for each
type of supplies and materials, utilizing the prescribed format as
outlined in Annexes 5 of Volume II of the New Government
Accounting System (NGAS) Manual for Local Government Units
(LGUs);

d. Municipal Accountant shall assign the appropriate account codes
with each journal entry in the disbursement vouchers; and

e. Municipal Accountant to use the prescribed form for Report on
Checks Issued with reference to Annex 47 of Volume II NGAS-
LGUs.

6.15. We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to recognize
the accrual of Leave Benefits Payable in the books of accounts upon
determination of the cost or monetary value of the accumulated leave
credits for the year.

6.16. We also recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to disclose
in the financial statements the details and financial impact of the
impending full and retroactive recognition of the cost or monetary value
of the accumulated leave credits of all municipal personnel while
awaiting clear budgeting guidelines on the matter.

7.12. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the:

a. Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to conduct
regular reconciliation of records of RPT and SET; and

b. Municipal Accountant to draw appropriate journal entries to
reflect the accurate balances of the tax receivables and their
corresponding deferred tax income accounts.

8.11. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to undertake the
following actions:

a. Send written notices to the payees to verify the existence of stale
checks particularly those issued in the prior years and prepare a
replacement check upon submission of the original check from the
payee;

b. Improve monitoring of check releases and adopt the practice of
sending written notices to payees at least one month before the checks
become stale; and

c. Prepare Journal Entry Vouchers (JEV) for all stale checks in order to
properly present the cash in bank account and to recognize the
appropriate liability in the Financial Statements.

9.16. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor, the members of the MDC,
the MPDC and other Municipal Officials concerned agreed to:

a. Ensure that priority development projects to be funded out of the
20% DF are well-planned and procurement and implementation-
ready;

b. Incorporate in the annual investment plan the specific periods of
implementation of the corresponding PPAs, serving as a reference for
the implementing offices to initiate implementation of the projects;

c. Conduct periodic reviews on the implementation of projects funded
under the 20% Development Fund in order for the Management to
take necessary courses of action in ensuring optimum utilization of
resources, timely completion of projects, and alignment with
development priorities; and

d. Implement the priority development projects programmed for the
year to ensure optimum utilization of the 20% DF in compliance with
the provisions of DBM-DOF-DILG JMC No. 1 series of 2020.

10.16. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to undertake the
following actions:

a. Direct the LDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant to revisit the
allocation of the LDRRMF and to utilize the fund for disaster
management related PPAs in accordance with the JMC No. 2013-1;

b. Direct the LDRRMO and the Municipal Accountant to charge and
utilize administrative related expenses under the MOOE of the
General Fund or the Solid Waste Management Fund, where
appropriate; and

c. Ensure that disbursement vouchers are supported with complete
documentation before processing for payment of claims.

11.9. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the GAD
Focal Person to prepare the Municipality’s three-year GAD Agenda to
establish the Municipality’s strategic framework and plan on gender
mainstreaming, women’s empowerment, and gender equality which
should be the basis in the preparation of the annual GPB; and inform
the DILG-Regional Office in writing of any changes or additional PPAs
that need to be implemented relevant to the current gender issues or
GAD-related undertakings, in compliance with the relevant provisions
of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

12.9. We recommended and the Local School Board agreed to submit the
Quarterly and Annual Reports of SEF Utilization to the DepEd CO,
through appropriate channels, not later than the 20th day after the end
of each quarter and the 15th day of February of the following year,
respectively, copy furnished the local sanggunian concerned and the
Regional Offices of the DBM and DILG.

13.12. We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the
Municipal Accountant to:

a. Submit the paid disbursement vouchers and their supporting
documents totaling ₱10.068 million, and 102 cancelled checks, to the
Office of the Auditor for post-audit and verification or accounting
thereof; and

b. Ensure that the submission of disbursement vouchers and
supporting documents thereto is made promptly and observe strictly
the provisions of Section 347, Book II of Republic Act No. 7160 and
Section 4 of P.D. No. 1445.

CY 2021 - Discrepancy of ₱7.438 million between the financial statements and Inventory Report of the General Services Office (GSO)
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the General Services Officer to:

a.	Conduct the physical count of PPE in accordance with the procedural guidelines enumerated in COA Circular No. 2020-006;

b.	Classify assets that did not meet the capitalization threshold of ₱50,000.00; and

c.	Provide clear description of PPEs in order to aid the proper identification, as well as, the correct classification of these accounts.

We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:

a.	Prepare adjusting entry/ies to reclassify the assets that did not meet the capitalization threshold of ₱50,000.00;

b.	Ensure that the transactions are properly classified in accordance with the prescribed Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units;

c.	Review the supporting schedules, identify those items that were mis-classified and prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the accounts and their balances in the books; and

d.	Identify PPEs that have already exhausted their estimated useful life in order to aid Management’s decision whether these will be disposed of or continued to be used in the operations of the Municipality.

Furthermore, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor submit a request to the COA RO VI-Technical Services for the review and appraisal of the unserviceable properties.

We recommended that Management:

Ensure that all PPEs included in the Inventory Report are duly recorded in the Property Cards (PCs) maintained by the GSO and reconciled with the PPELCs maintained by the Accounting Unit in accordance with 6.3 of COA Circular No. 2020-006.

Exert effort to identify or itemize the properties that were recorded as beginning balances of PPE.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, HRMO and other department heads concerned to institute measures/system in order to manage and bring the monthly net take home pay to the minimum level set by the General Appropriations Act. This may include prioritizing in the payrolls those deductions and contributions mandated by law and ensuring full payment of certain loans before securing another.
observations.reason:
Several personnel/
employees still have monthly net take home pay of less than ₱5,000.00.

We further recommended and Management agreed to the complete and prompt submission of monthly bank reconciliation statements to verify the accuracy of cash in bank balances reflected in the books of the Municipality and in the depository banks.

We reiterated to recommend and the Municipal Mayor agreed to:

Stop using the Twenty Percent Development Fund for the payment of wages of job hires in compliance with Section 2.3 of DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2017-1;

Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) of the General Fund the payment of wages of job hires so that the 20% Development Fund could be properly utilized to finance the implementation of developmental projects that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures; and

Return the amount taken from the twenty percent development fund spent for the payment of wages of job hires.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

Stop using the 20% Development Fund to finance the repair and maintenance of Municipal structures and payment of wages of job hires in compliance with Sections 2.3 of DILG-DBM Joint Circular No. 2017-1 dated February 22, 2017.

Direct the Municipal Budget Officer to include in the Maintenance and Other Operating Expenses (MOOE) of the General Fund the repair and maintenance of Municipal structures and payment of wages of job hires so that the 20% Development Fund could be properly utilized to finance the implementation of developmental projects that partake the nature of investment or capital expenditures which are more beneficial to the public and have long term benefits.

Return the amount taken from the 20% Development Fund spent for the repair and maintenance of Municipal structures and payment of wages of job hires.

We likewise recommended that those projects/programs with general use or having administrative nature must be appropriated/funded out of the General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the designated personnel or Supply Officer of the Municipality to submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and to ensure that the driver’s trip tickets are properly accomplished.

We recommend that the LGU GAD Focal Point System composed of an Executive Committee and Technical Working Group adhere to the provisions prescribed under the Philippine Commission on Women (PCW)-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013 to ensure compliance in mainstreaming gender perspectives in local planning, programming, budgeting, project development, implementation, monitoring and evaluation.
observations.reason: Lack of funds for the implementation of GAD PPA. Increase of local revenue collection is proposed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the MSWDO furnish a copy of the final ESA master list duly verified and validated by the DSWD RO VI to the office of the auditor for reference in validating the names of the beneficiaries especially those with alterations in the payroll.

We also recommend that all beneficiaries should affix their signature/thumb marks on the payroll.

We recommend that the LGU refrain from charging similar expenses with no capital expenditure property or components or of administrative or regular activities from the 20% Development Fund to ensure that concrete, meaningful development projects, program and activities could be realized with long-term benefits, and to avoid disallowance in audit for future charging of similar items.

We also recommend that those projects/programs with general use or having administrative nature may be appropriated/funded out of the General Fund Proper.
observations.reason: The Municipality is Internal Revenue Allotment (IRA) dependent. Some disbursements form the 20% Development Fund will be gradually transferred to Other MOOE.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the MDRRMO to adhere strictly with the provision of Item 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 and submit regularly to the COA Audit Team of the Municipality the monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund on or before the 15th day after the end of each month through the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council and the Municipal Development Council. Relative thereto, the LGU Accountant is advised to prepare promptly the said report to the MDRRMO.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower/ personnel.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that the Municipal Accountant take up depreciation, or impairment loss in the new Chart of Accounts, for the foregoing PPE accounts totaling P2,336,992.72. 

We also recommend that depreciation expenses be recorded periodically in compliance with the above regulations and for the purpose of systematic and rational allocation of non-cash expenses over the assets’ useful lives. Proper recording and valuation of these assets would provide meaningful presentation of PPE accounts in the LGU’s financial statements.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the designated personnel or Supply Officer of the Municipality to submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and to ensure that the driver’s trip tickets are properly accomplished.
observations.managementAction: Lack of personnel. Municipal Mayor to designate personnel to prepare the monthly report on fuel consumption.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer deposit intact with AGDB, the full amount received and collected daily or the next banking day pursuant to Section 69 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 and Section 32 of Manual on the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units (LGUs), Volume I, in order to avoid loss/misuse of such funds.
observations.reason: Reiterated on this year’s report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that copy of any contract, letter order, job order or purchase order, regardless of the amount, and all its supporting documents be submitted to the Auditor concerned within five (5) days from issuance.
observations.reason: Delayed submission of MOAs, POs/LOs/JOs and other form of Agreement.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to require the liquidation of the cash advances of concerned officials and employees. Moreover demand letters should be sent to concerned employees and officials. The Local Chief executive may cause the suspension of payment of salaries of concerned employees and officials until full liquidation of the cash advance.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor instruct the concerned Municipal Officials to remit the unutilized Priority Development Assistance Fund (PDAF) totaling P129,201.82 to the Bureau of Treasury pursuant to the Supreme Court Decision declaring the said fund to be unconstitutional.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer remitted the unutilized PDAF to the Bureau of Treasury last August 5, 2016.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to review and verify properly and thoroughly the supporting documents attached for claims charged against the SEF to establish the propriety, legality and conformity of the claims with the provisions of Section 1 of Republic Act No. 5447, Section 4.0 of DBM and DILG Joint Circular No. 01s. 1998, dated April 14, 1998, and Section 2.1 of DECS, DBM, and DILG Joint Circular No. 01-A, dated June 25, 2001.
observations.reason: Reiterated on this year’s report.

observations.recommendation: We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer monitor and see to it that all ORs issued by the collecting officers are issued in numerical sequence.
observations.managementAction: Memorandum Order were already issued to revenue collectors.
